regions.eastern-caribbean: Easterly winds at 15-18 knots are expected to prevail across the Eastern Caribbean region, including ports such as San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Maarten, over the next three days. Partly cloudy skies with scattered rain showers are anticipated, with the highest chance of rain, 55%, on Day 1. Cruise passengers and yacht captains should be prepared for increasing winds and potential rain showers, especially when navigating through channels and anchorages around St. Kitts and Antigua. Waters are expected to remain moderate, with no significant wave height or marine wind warnings issued at this time.
regions.western-caribbean: Current weather conditions in the Western Caribbean, including ports such as Cozumel, Costa Maya, and Roatan, are partly cloudy with southeast winds at 10 knots. A 3-day outlook indicates increasing rain chances with thunderstorms expected, particularly on Day 3 with a 73% chance of rain and winds shifting to the north-northeast at 19 knots. Cruise passengers and yacht captains should prepare for potential thunderstorms and rougher seas, especially when approaching or departing ports like Belize City and Grand Cayman. In anchorage areas, such as off the coast of Ocho Rios and Falmouth, mariners should exercise caution due to potential squalls and gusty winds.
regions.bahamas: Current conditions in the Bahamas are mostly overcast with light southeast winds at 11 knots and a high temperature of 80F. For the next three days, expect increasing winds and thunderstorm activity, particularly on Day 3 when a cold front moves into the area, bringing a 74% chance of rain and north-northeast winds at 13 knots. Cruise passengers and yacht captains should exercise caution when navigating the waters around Nassau, Freeport, and Bimini, and be prepared for potential rain showers and gusty winds, especially when approaching or departing these ports. Fishing captains should also be aware of the changing wind and sea conditions, particularly in the Berry Islands and Turks & Caicos, and plan their routes and anchorage accordingly to ensure safe navigation.
regions.southern-caribbean: Expect partly cloudy skies with east winds at 17-22 knots in the Southern Caribbean region, including ports Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire, with a slight chance of rain showers over the next three days. Cruise passengers and yacht captains can anticipate moderate sea conditions, while fishing captains should be prepared for choppy waters, particularly near Cartagena. No significant tropical weather systems are present in the area, but occasional rain showers may develop, especially on Day 1 with a 35% chance of rain.
regions.central-caribbean: Southeasterly winds at 11-15 knots will prevail across the Central Caribbean region, including ports such as Roatan, Belize City, and Grand Cayman, over the next three days. Cruise passengers and yacht captains should expect partly cloudy conditions with increasing rain chances, reaching 58% by day three, particularly in the Cozumel and Costa Maya areas. Mariners are advised to monitor weather updates and prepare for potential rain showers and gusty winds, especially when navigating through anchorage areas. No significant tropical weather systems or marine wind warnings are currently forecast for the region.
regions.lesser-antilles: Current weather conditions in the Lesser Antilles region, including Barbados, indicate east-northeast winds of 20 knots with rain showers and a 26% chance of precipitation. Over the next three days, winds will remain east-northeast at 20-22 knots, with an increased chance of rain showers on Day 1, reaching 80%. Cruise passengers and yacht captains can expect partly cloudy conditions on Days 2 and 3, with decreased precipitation chances. When navigating ports such as St. Lucia, Martinique, and St. Maarten, be prepared for potential rain showers and gusty winds, and exercise caution when anchoring in exposed areas.
regions.los-angeles: Current conditions in the Los Angeles region are favorable with clear skies and light winds at 6kt from the SSW. Over the next three days, fog is expected to move in, with temperatures rising to 71F on Day 1 and 83F on Day 2, accompanied by winds from the SSW and W at 8-9kt. Cruise passengers and yacht captains can expect smooth sailing in the ports of Los Angeles, Long Beach, and Marina del Rey, but should be prepared for reduced visibility due to fog. Fishing captains operating near Catalina Island should exercise caution when navigating through the foggy conditions.
regions.ensenada: Current conditions in Ensenada are favorable with clear skies and light winds at 6kt from the WSW. For the next three days, expect foggy conditions to prevail with temperatures ranging from 64F to 86F and winds remaining relatively light at 6-8kt from the SW. Cruise passengers and yacht captains visiting Punta Banda or anchoring near Islas Todos Santos should be prepared for reduced visibility due to fog, while fishing captains should take note of the calm sea conditions. No significant weather advisories are expected for the region.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz ports can expect pleasant weather over the next 3 days with temperatures ranging from 72F to 74F and partly cloudy skies. Northwest winds at 11-14kt will lead to moderate sea conditions, and anchorage conditions will be favorable with no significant wave height concerns. Cruise passengers and yacht captains should prepare for gentle breezes and clear skies, making it ideal for outdoor activities and water sports, while fishing captains can take advantage of the calm seas to venture out into the Gulf of California.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region ports, including Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata, can expect partly cloudy to overcast conditions with light to moderate winds over the next 3 days. Northwest winds at 9-10 knots on Days 1 and 2 will give way to west-southwest winds at 7 knots on Day 3. With zero percent chance of rain, cruise passengers and yacht captains can plan for smooth sailing and anchorage conditions. Fishing captains should be aware of the gentle winds and partly cloudy skies, ideal for navigating the waters off these ports.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Cruise passengers and yacht captains can expect pleasant weather in the Puerto Vallarta region, with partly cloudy skies and light winds of 7kt from the west. Anchorage conditions at Punta Mita and Chacala will be calm, with no significant wave height concerns. Over the next three days, temperatures will remain steady, ranging from 79-81F, with no chance of rain, making it ideal for water activities and shore excursions in Puerto Vallarta and Yelapa.
regions.western-mediterranean: Calm conditions prevail in the Western Mediterranean, with partly cloudy skies and light winds expected to continue over the next three days. Cruise passengers and yacht captains can expect pleasant weather in ports such as Barcelona, Valencia, and Palma de Mallorca, with temperatures ranging from 62-65F. Fog may develop in some areas on Day 3, potentially reducing visibility in anchorage areas like Gibraltar and Cadiz, so mariners should exercise caution when navigating these regions.
regions.central-mediterranean: Cruise passengers and yacht captains can expect mild temperatures in the Central Mediterranean region, with highs reaching 67F in ports like Naples and Marseille. Winds will be moderate, ranging from 5-13kt, with a slight increase in rain chances over the next three days, particularly in areas like Livorno and Genoa. Mariners should exercise caution when navigating through the Corsica and Sardinia channels due to potential rain showers and gusty winds. In ports like Split and Dubrovnik, anchorage conditions will remain relatively calm, but crews should be prepared for potential changes in wind direction and speed.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Fair weather is expected in the Eastern Mediterranean region for the next three days, with partly cloudy to overcast skies and light northeast winds. Cruise passengers and yacht captains can anticipate smooth sailing conditions, with wave heights remaining relatively low, making for ideal anchorage conditions in ports such as Santorini and Mykonos. However, fishing captains should be aware of increasing winds and cloud cover by Day 3, potentially leading to reduced visibility, especially in areas around Rhodes and Corfu. No significant weather advisories are currently in effect for the region, but mariners should continue to monitor local forecasts for any updates.
